data.title: Zscaler's AI innovations raise bar for cybersecurity: analysts
data.description:
Zscaler (NASDAQ:ZS) held its annual cybersecurity conference, Zenith Live 2025, this week in Las Vegas, and unveiled new artificial intelligence innovations that impressed analysts in attendance.

Shares were up 2.6% during early market action on Wednesday.

During the event, Zscaler highlighted its new advanced AI security capabilities and new AI-powered innovations to enhance data security and stop cyberattacks.

"In our view, new solutions extending Zero Trust Everywhere are the more immediate consideration when thinking about potential benefits to the Zscaler model," said Needham analysts, led by Mike Cikos, in a Wednesday investor note. "We believe AI-powered capabilities demonstrate the strong, consistent innovation engine at Zscaler, especially Prompt Visibility and Segmentation."

Needham also mentioned Z-Flex, a "new procurement plan launched in late April which has already amassed more than $65 Million in TCV Bookings (including one Fortune 500 Technology Customer which grew spend more than 40% to $19 Million in ARR)."

Needham reiterated its Buy rating and increased its price target to $350 from $310.

BTIG also reiterated its Buy rating and upped its price target to $329 from $298. 

"During the event, we gained a better understanding of ZS's play into the SecOps/SIEM space with Red Canary," said BTIG analysts Gray Powell and Trevor Rambo, in an investor note. "To start, ZS has (quietly) invested $1B+ in the SecOps space over the last year, mainly through the acquisition of Avalor and Red Canary and through internal R&D. The product will not replace existing SIEMs. But ZS plans to target SIEM budget dollars and solve complex SIEM use cases."

Similarly, Wedbush reiterated its Outperform rating and raised its price target to $330 from $300.

"ZS' new products are continuing to see strong demand and is resonating with customers as it continues to grow at a faster rate than the rest of the product portfolio making up $1+ billion in ARR (~1/3 of total ARR)," said Wedbush analysts, led by Daniel Ives, in a Wednesday note.

data.title: Lucid signs supply deal with Graphite One for U.S.-sourced battery material
data.description:
Lucid Group (NASDAQ:LCID) said Wednesday it signed a multi-year supply agreement for U.S.-processed graphite with Graphite One, strengthening the company's supply chain for American raw materials and resources following other recent agreements with Graphite One and Syrah Resources (OTCPK:SYAAF).

Graphite One will supply Lucid (NASDAQ:LCID) and its battery cell suppliers with natural graphite, expected to begin production in 2028, sourced from the Graphite Creek deposit north of Nome, Alaska.

Lucid (LCID) said the latest agreement builds on last year's announcement with Graphite One to provide synthetic graphite for future vehicles starting in 2028, which will be sourced from Graphite One's proposed active anode material facility in Warren, Ohio.

Under a third agreement, Syrah Resource (OTCPK:SYAAF) is set to supply natural graphite active anode material to Lucid (LCID) with foreign material processed in Louisiana.

Lucid (LCID) said the agreements to source natural and synthetic graphite continue its focus on strengthening its U.S.-based supply chain and establishing local supply for critical minerals; graphite, both synthetic and natural, composes a significant amount of the material within lithium-ion batteries and is essential for fast-charging performance.

data.title: Guidewire surges after 'strong' Q3 results draw praise from analysts
data.description:
Shares of Guidewire Software (NYSE:GWRE) soared nearly 16% premarket on Wednesday after third quarter fiscal 2025 results surpassed estimates, which drew praise from analysts.

BofA Securities reiterated its Underperform rating but increased the price target on Guidewire's stock to $160 from $135  

Analysts led Michael Funk said that insurance software vendor Guidewire "reported strong 3Q25 results, with beats on all major metrics and positive revisions to revenue, ARR [annual recurring revenue], operating income, and CFO guidance."

The analysts said key positives from the quarter include — seven core system deals with Tier 1 (very large) insurers; 17 closed cloud deals; and 10 go-lives.

Guidewire Management's commentary surrounding customer scrutiny on near-term investments/budgets was incrementally more positive than in previous quarters, the analysts added.

Funk and his team noted that the company remains slightly ahead of schedule in its progression towards its longer-term margin targets, and provided relatively constructive commentary on gross margin trajectory.

"While we find very little to nitpick in the company’s 3Q25 results, we remain cautious on the risk/reward as the stock trades at an expensive 13x CY26E EV/Rev (among the highest in software)," said the analysts.

Wells Fargo Securities kept its Overweight rating and raised the price target to $265 from $220.

Analysts led by Michael Turrin said Guidewire delivered a "healthy beat & raise" across all key metrics with cloud momentum continuing to trend favorably into seasonally strong fiscal fourth quarter.

Related stocks: Sapiens International (SPNS) and SAP (SAP) were largely flat premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Western Union to provide cross-border money transfer services across Post Office branches
data.description:
International money transfers company Western Union (NYSE:WU) has agreed on a long-term exclusive deal with the Post Office for cross-border money transfer services at the latter's branches.

WU is set to be the sole international money transfer services provider across the Post Office network.

As part of the deal, postmasters and strategic partners are expected to benefit from enhanced financial incentives.

data.title: VerticalScope appoints Chris Goodridge as CEO
data.description:
• VerticalScope (TSX:FORA:CA) announced on Wednesday the appointment of Chris Goodridge as chief executive officer, succeeding VerticalScope founder Rob Laidlaw, who will continue in his role as Chair of the Board. I
  • In addition, Ezra Menaged, previously CEO of Hometalk, has been promoted to chief operating officer, said the company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Canada's Composite and Services PMI rise in May
data.description:
• Composite PMI in Canada increased to 45.50 points in May from 41.70 points in April 2025.

  • Services PMI in Canada increased to 45.60 points in May from 41.50 points in April of 2025. 

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA).
  • Currency: (USD:CAD) (USD:CAD)

data.title: CoreWeave appoints Magnetar's Ernie Rogers as chief architect of strategic financing
data.description:
"AI Hyperscaler" CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) announced on Wednesday the appointment of Ernie Rogers as chief architect of strategic financing.

Shares were +3.40% pre-market to $155.59.

Most recently, Rogers served as COO at the hedge fund company Magnetar Capital, where he led the firm's operations and finances.
